name: Swedish Rye Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Scald the milk and combine it with the water and brown sugar in a very large bowl. You need something that holds at least 4 or 5 quarts.
2. When the mixture is lukewarm, dissolve the yeast in it.
3. Stir in 2 cups rye flour and 1 cup white flour to make a paste.
4. Let the mixture rise in a warm place until it is light and foamy. This usually takes about 30 minutes to an hour.
5. Stir in the granulated sugar, oil, molasses, anise seed, and salt. Add enough flour to make a stiff dough using 2 parts rye to 1 part white flour.
6. Knead the dough for about 5 minutes, or until it is smooth and elastic, adding more flour to keep it from sticking to your hands.
7. Clean and grease the bowl. Put the dough in the bowl, turning it to grease all sides. Cover the bowl loosely with a clean towel and let the dough rise until doubled in bulk.
8. Punch down the dough and let it rise until doubled again.
9. Divide the dough into three loaves and put them in greased pans. Bake on cookie sheets for about 45 minutes at 350°F. Because of the high sugar content, this bread can burn rather easily; watch it closely so it doesn't get too dark.
